title: More on Classical Stability of Hopf-like Solitons of the Toroidal-Twisted type
abstract: The Faddeev-Hopf model [1] supporting Hopfions was shown to emerge in the low-energy limit of four-dimensional scalar quantum electrodynamics (QED) with two charged scalar fields [2, 3]. Faddeev and Noemi conjectured that the Hopfions and Hopf-like solitons -- vortons -- can be based on a twisted toroidal structure inherent to QED [4-6]. This conjecture was discussed in detail in [2] in the approximation of negligibly small extrinsic curvature. Qualitative and semi-quantitative arguments were used to demonstrate the validity of the Faddeev-Noemi hypothesis. Here we further enhance the proof by applying a numerical analysis which confirms that large-size Hopf-like solitons exist as local energy minima in the full QED theory (in the Faddeev-Skyrme model they become topological solitons representing the global minima in the given topological sector).
summary: This paper provides numerical evidence that twisted, toroidal-type solitons (vortons) are classically stable in a 4D scalar QED-derived model. It demonstrates that these structures reach a stable equilibrium length, preventing them from collapsing or expanding indefinitely. This confirms a long-standing conjecture regarding the existence of stable Hopf-like solitons in these field theories.
main_problem: Investigating the classical stability of Hopf-like solitons (vortons) of the toroidal-twisted type and determining if they are dynamically stabilized at a finite equilibrium length.
main_result: Numerical analysis confirms that large-size Hopf-like solitons exist as local energy minima in the full QED theory, with the twisted string being dynamically stabilized at a finite equilibrium length L*.
method: Numerical analysis using a Python-based implementation of a 4th-order finite difference stencil and scipy.optimize to solve nonlinear equations of motion.
model_or_system: A deformed O(3) nonlinear sigma model (Faddeev-Hopf model) emerging from the low-energy limit of 4-dimensional scalar quantum electrodynamics (QED) with two charged scalar fields.
key_observables: Total energy E, equilibrium length L*, soliton mass M_sol, and the Hopf-like topological invariant h = kn.
important_parameters: The parameter beta (controlling the fourth-order term), winding numbers n (azimuthal) and k (longitudinal), and the scale hierarchy L* >> rho >> 1/m_gamma.
main_assumptions: The analysis uses a twisted string ansatz in an R2 x S1 geometry, assuming negligible extrinsic curvature (large-loop limit) and a gauge field constraint where the current C = 0.
figures_summary: Figure 3 shows energy E vs. length L for various beta values; Figure 4 shows soliton mass growth with beta; Figure 6 shows radial energy density distribution; Figure 7 shows energy dependence based on previous analytic estimates.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the Faddeev-Hopf model and the vorton conjecture, presents the numerical methodology and discretization, details the energy minimization results and parameter dependence, compares findings with existing literature (Jäykkä-Speight and BDS models), and discusses stability and limitations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: The simplest Hopf-like soliton as a string with the α twist illustrating the Faddeev-Niemi hypothesis [1]. A Belavin-Polyakov “instanton” on the plane AB is extended in one extra dimension ℓand bent into a torus, with a 2π twist of the instanton phase modulus.
Figure 2: The twist of the angular modulus, with k = 7.
Figure 3: String energy versus the length of the string for n = 1, g = 1, m = 10.
Figure 5: Profiles of the twisted vortex for n = 1, g = 1, m = 10
Figure 6: String energy distribution over the radial coordinate for n = 1, g = 1, m = 10
